text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management involves breaking large tasks into smaller steps and setting personal milestones. Prioritization helps you focus on what is most important first, rather than getting lost in less urgent details. Scope management means knowing when to say no or adjust expectations to avoid overcommitting. Finally, clear communication with your team or supervisor is crucial; if a deadline becomes impossible, it is better to explain early and propose a solution than to deliver late without warning. By balancing these elements, you can meet deadlines more consistently and reduce stress.
